name: Chicken Piccata
recipeInstructions:
1. Remove bones and skin from chicken breasts.
2. Gently pound the meat with a mallet until thin and flat but not broken.
3. Sprinkle meat with black pepper and dredge lightly in flour.
4. Preheat a heavy wide skillet over medium-high heat.
5. Add olive oil to the skillet and sauté garlic until lightly browned, then remove and set aside.
6. Increase heat to high and fry chicken quickly, turning as needed until golden brown on both sides. Remove and set aside.
7. Drain excess oil from the pan, then deglaze with white wine, adding demi-glace (if using), lemon juice, and reserved garlic. Stir well to heat the sauce thoroughly.
8. Add butter to the sauce. Optionally remove the garlic with a slotted spoon or leave it in.
9. Garnish chicken with chopped parsley and serve immediately.
This dish is excellent when prepared with Chukar, pheasant, Hungarian partridge, blue grouse, or ruffed grouse. The demi-glace can be omitted for a lighter sauce.
